verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window

best.windFit: SSW 35 km/h · cross-off · too windy, strong

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 18 kJ, 12 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 10-13s at low tide can still work well on Belgas, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind is over 30 km/h; wind fit is poor or too strong; gusts over 45 km/h add chop risk.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind fit is poor or too strong
gusts over 45 km/h add chop risk
